bindings.quest_reward_hint: a lesser work of the smith

dialogueChunks.intro: The blacksmith hasn't left the forge in eleven days. His family moved out a month ago. He sold the furniture last week to buy more ore.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: He's been chasing {quest_focus} for three years. Every coin, every favor, every relationship: all burned in the furnace alongside the metal. His latest supply of rare ore was stolen by {enemy_type} raiders from {quest_origin}, and now he has nothing left to work with. Nothing except the obsession.
dialogueChunks.outro: His wife asked me to find someone to {quest_action} before {quest_threat} finishes what the obsession started. She still cares, even from a distance. But the ore is deep in {enemy_type} territory.

playerResponses.text: I'll get the ore. Maybe finishing the blade will set him free.
Maybe he needs to lose the ore. Some obsessions need to end.
I can't solve a man's war with himself.

dialogueChunks.intro: There's an alchemist holed up in {quest_origin} who has been brewing something for the better part of a year. Everyone who knew her has either been driven away or bought out.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: She sold her home, her practice, and her reputation to fund the research on {quest_focus}. The last batch required reagents so rare she traded away her guild certification to get them. Then the {enemy_type} raided her supply cache. {quest_stakes}: gone overnight.
dialogueChunks.outro: She won't stop. She can't. If someone doesn't {quest_action} from the raider camp, she'll try something desperate with substitutes, and {quest_threat} could level the tower and everything around it.

playerResponses.text: I'd rather face raiders than an exploding tower. I'll go.
Sounds like she needs an intervention, not an errand runner.
Let her face the consequences of her choices.

dialogueChunks.intro: The merchant used to be the wealthiest person in three settlements. Now he sleeps in his vault because he sold the bed.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: He's spent everything on {quest_focus}: relics, tablets, fragments of a civilization nobody else remembers. His children haven't spoken to him in years. His business partners have all been bought out or abandoned. He needs one more piece, the keystone that makes sense of everything, and he swears the obsession will end.
dialogueChunks.outro: His eldest asked me to find someone to {quest_action}. Not to enable the addiction, but because {quest_time_pressure} and if the collection isn't complete, he'll have destroyed {quest_stakes} for an incomplete puzzle. At least let it mean something.

playerResponses.text: If finding it ends this, I'll look. Where was it last seen?
You realize finding it probably won't stop him.
